_comment: Tier-1 objective gates derived from JTBD-4 (1-requirements.md). Video expectations are graded by viewing extracted keyframes of the actual MP4s - never from filenames or text descriptions.

evals.prompt: Generate the clips and assemble the episode for the project ikea-deadline from its plan and stills log.
evals.expected_output: artifacts/ikea-deadline/04-clips/ with one MP4 per kept beat, an assembled artifacts/ikea-deadline/episode.mp4 (15-60s, planned aspect, beats in order, room tone per plan), and 05-summary.md reporting per clip the model, dialect, prompt, duration, and any fallbacks.
expectations: artifacts/ikea-deadline/04-clips/ contains one MP4 per kept beat in plan order (NN-<beat-slug>.mp4)
artifacts/ikea-deadline/episode.mp4 exists, is playable, and ffprobe-reported duration is 15-60s at the planned aspect ratio (the verification output is recorded)
05-summary.md lists for EVERY clip: model used, dialect (single-shot or seedance), composed prompt, duration, and fallbacks taken; episode-level audio treatment and limitations are stated plainly
video model discovery ran first (ai-gen models --type video) and the chosen dialect follows from what was actually routed - never the CLI default model
the episode's beats appear in plan order and the character/sketch style visibly persists across clips (verified by viewing extracted keyframes)

evals.prompt: For the project missing-url-test, beat 2 has no fal.media URL in the stills log. Generate clips and assemble the episode anyway.
evals.expected_output: Beat 2 becomes a still-as-segment (subtle zoom on the local still) so the episode keeps every beat; the fallback is flagged in 05-summary.md.
expectations: the episode contains every beat including beat 2 (as a still-as-segment), not a silently shortened cut
05-summary.md explicitly flags beat 2 as a still-segment fallback with the reason (missing hosted URL)
